message:
franken_numpy-vbpx expose numpy.dtypes submodule (33 concrete DType classes)

Mirror numpy.dtypes on fnp_python.dtypes: eager re-export from
numpy.dtypes when importable, PEP-562 lazy fallback for embedded
interpreters, and an explicit 33-name fallback __all__ covering the
full integer / floating / complex / object / string / void / temporal
DType class set published in numpy 2.x:
- Boolean: BoolDType
- Signed int (5 widths × {primary, alias}): Int8/Byte/Int16/Short/
  Int32/Int/Int64/Long/LongLong DType
- Unsigned int: UInt8/UByte/UInt16/UShort/UInt32/UInt/UInt64/ULong/
  ULongLong DType
- Floating: Float16/Float32/Float64/LongDouble DType
- Complex: Complex64/Complex128/CLongDouble DType
- Other: ObjectDType, BytesDType, StrDType, VoidDType,
  DateTime64DType, TimeDelta64DType, StringDType

Add dtypes_submodule_matches_numpy parity test:
- __all__ non-empty, includes every canonical 33-name entry, and
  equals numpy.dtypes's __all__ verbatim (order preserved).
- Each name resolves to the SAME class object as numpy.dtypes.<name>
  so isinstance(dt, fnp_python.dtypes.X) classifies identically.
- PEP-562 lazy-fallback verification: drop each eager attr and
  re-getattr; each name must still resolve through numpy.dtypes.

rch lib suite: 475/475 pass. UBS exit 0.
